mnemonics.title: Remember Quantifier Order Matters
mnemonics.content: FOLQ: For All Quantifier comes before Exists - Order dictates meaning. ∀x∃y means 'for each x, there exists y' but ∃y∀x means 'there exists a y for all x' - completely different!

mnemonics.title: Free vs Bound Variables
mnemonics.content: FREE as in 'not caught'; BOUND as in 'captured by quantifier'. Free variables 'roam free' without restrictions; bound variables are 'bound' by ∀ or ∃.

mnemonics.title: Term Definition
mnemonics.content: TERMS: T = Variables ∪ Constants ∪ {f(t1,...,tn) | f is function, ti are terms}. Remember: Terms are 'nouns' - they name objects; Formulas are 'sentences' that can be true/false.

mnemonics.title: De Morgan's Laws for Quantifiers
mnemonics.content: NEGATE: Not ForAll = Exists Not; Not Exists = ForAll Not. ¬∀x = ∃x¬ ; ¬∃x = ∀x¬. Swap the quantifier and negate the predicate!

acronyms.usage: A logical system with quantifiers, predicates, variables, and functions for reasoning about objects and their properties.
A syntactically correct formula in logic. Defined recursively: atoms → connectives/quantifiers → WFFs.
The non-empty set of objects that quantifiers range over. All variables, constants refer to elements in D.
I = (D, Ic, If, Ip) - assigns meaning: Domain D, constants to elements, functions to operations, predicates to relations.
The set of non-logical symbols: constant symbols, function symbols, and predicate symbols used in a theory.

visualTips.visualization: Draw boxes around quantifier scopes. ∀x [ -------- scope of ∀x -------- ]. Variables inside the box matching x are bound. This visual 'captures' the variable.
Think of interpretation like a dictionary: Domain = vocabulary set, Constants = proper nouns, Functions = operations/methods, Predicates = adjectives/verbs describing relationships.
Use a truth table tree: Root = formula, Branches based on subformulas, Leaves = atomic evaluations. For quantified formulas, check each element in domain.
Build syntax tree: Leaves = atomic formulas/predicates, Internal nodes = connectives/quantifiers. Traversing bottom-up shows how formula is constructed; top-down shows how to evaluate.

analogies.analogy: Terms are like nouns (John, x, Father(John)) - they name things. Formulas are like sentences (Loves(John,x)) - they make claims that can be true or false.
∀x: 'Every student passed' = All elements in domain (students) satisfy 'passed'. ∃x: 'Some student passed' = At least one element satisfies 'passed'.
Interpretation is like setting the 'rules of the game': Domain = what objects exist, symbols = what they mean. Without interpretation, formulas are just meaningless symbols.
Bound variables are like local variables in a function - they only exist within that scope. Free variables are like global variables - they can be accessed and changed anywhere.
Interpretation assigns values; Model is like a teacher checking if answer is correct. I ⊨ φ means interpretation 'passes' the formula test (formula is true).
